headline: Countries-ceiling Night 5: composite-100 ceiling artifact + April 16, 2026 Government proposal to expedite deportation enforcement and impose pre-emptive entry bans + Border Security Act extension to end-2026 (eastern-border asylum pushback)
methodology_notes: Countries-Ceiling Night 5 — fifth consecutive correction (Norway -15.3 April 22, Germany -23.1 April 23, Netherlands -21.5 April 23, Luxembourg -16.2 April 24, Iceland -12.5 April 24). Average prior-correction delta: -17.7. Finland delta -15.6 sits squarely in this range. Genuine Exemplary-band substrate retained: universal healthcare via Kela; comprehensive social-protection floor; high social trust; treaty-body engagement; tripartite labour-market governance. ACT lowered to 81.3 because the April 16, 2026 government proposal explicitly expedites enforcement of removal and adds pre-emptive entry bans before any permit history exists — a structural narrowing of asylum process protections. EQU lowered to 75.0 reflecting differential treatment of asylum-seekers under the Border Security Act (Russian-border pushback) which the Government extended to end-2026 by 168-29 vote (June 2024). INT lowered to 81.3 because Finland is consciously legislating policies that domestic legal scholars and Council of Europe bodies have flagged as in tension with non-refoulement and ECHR Article 3 obligations — values-divergence flag. The composite-100 published score remains an artifact of the original countries-index ceiling pattern; this correction removes that artifact while preserving Exemplary band consistent with substantive Finnish institutional record. Same calibration session as Denmark and Sweden (countries-ceiling Night 5 trio).

evidence.claim: April 16, 2026 — Finnish Government submitted to Parliament a proposal to expedite deportation enforcement and impose entry bans in advance (before permit history exists) on aliens deemed serious security risks
Finnish Migration Service confirmation: maximum detention period extended; legislation amends right-to-reside provisions following removal decisions

Border Security Act (Act on Temporary Measures to Combat Instrumentalized Migration) extended to end-2026 by Parliament vote of 168-29 — allows pushback of asylum-seekers at the closed eastern border with Russia, preventing asylum claim filing
Independent reporting confirms migration policy reform package as substantial restriction on asylum rights
Finland's universal healthcare via Kela, comprehensive social protection floor, and tripartite labour-market governance support Exemplary-band substrate
Published 100.0 is uniform 4.5×6+5×2=100 ceiling artifact shared with Denmark, Norway, Sweden — invalidated as evidence-based by prior 5 ceiling-corrections

key_evidence: April 16, 2026 government proposal to expedite deportation and impose pre-emptive entry bans (Parliament tabled)
Border Security Act extended to end-2026 (asylum pushback at Russian border)
Universal Kela healthcare, tripartite labour governance retain Exemplary-band substrate
EQU 75.0 reflects differential asylum-seeker treatment; INT 81.3 reflects values-divergence on non-refoulement
Composite-100 published score is countries-ceiling artifact (Night 5 of 5)
